$schemaVersion: 6.0 — extends v5/v4.1. Values in this file are strings describing each field's type and allowed values. NEW IN V6: structured content blocks (markdown text, code, callout, table, key-terms), per-block checkpoints, section summaries with prediction-resolution hooks, executed confidence calibration, missed-item review scheduling, executed adaptive difficulty, and the elaboration interaction type. All v6 additions are optional-safe: a v5 course file is a valid v6 course file.

courseMetadata.courseId: string — unique ID. Keys learner progress (course-progress:<courseId>). Changing it orphans existing saves.
courseMetadata.title: string
courseMetadata.version: string — course content version, displayed on completion seal
courseMetadata.schemaVersion: string — NEW v6, optional. '6.0'. Absent = pre-v6 course; player treats text blocks as format:'plain' unless block says otherwise.

learningModel.masteryThreshold: float 0–1 — default mastery bar; per-competency masteryThreshold overrides
learningModel.confidenceCalibrationEnabled: boolean — v6: ENFORCED. When true, interactions with confidencePrompt.enabled render the slider; the rating is stored with the answer and shown back in calibration feedback after reveal.
learningModel.confidenceSchedulingEnabled: boolean — NEW v6, optional, default false. When true, confidence modulates review scheduling: a correct answer given at low confidence (< 40% of scale) schedules a review at initialReviewDays instead of skipping the queue; an incorrect answer given at high confidence (> 80% of scale) is flagged 'miscalibrated' in the review item.
learningModel.adaptiveDifficultyEnabled: boolean — v6: ENFORCED for assessments that set adaptiveDifficulty:true. See assessments[].adaptiveRules.
learningModel.interleavingEnabled: boolean — v6: when true AND interleavingGroups exist, the player injects up to interleavingGroups[].injectCount retrieval items from sibling competencies into grouped experiences (between content and mastery blocks). Review queue always interleaves regardless.

interleavingGroups.injectCount: integer — NEW v6, optional, default 1. Max sibling retrieval items injected per experience in this group.
interleavingGroups.rationaleNote: string — author guidance, displayed on hover

learningModel.reviewOnMiss: boolean — NEW v6, optional, default false. When true, any auto-scored item answered incorrectly (interaction MC/ranking, probe MC, assessment MC) is synthesized into a review-queue item keyed by its questionId/interactionId, scheduled per missedItemSchedule. Written items self-rated Weak also enter the queue.

probeQuestions.prompt: string — markdown permitted (inline only: bold, italics, inline code)
probeQuestions.correctIndex: integer — required for multiple-choice; index into options[]

competencies.competencyId: string — the hub ID everything links to
competencies.title: string
competencies.taxonomyLevel: remember | understand | apply | analyze | evaluate | create

commonMisconceptions.misconceptionId: string — referenced by optionFeedback and errorPatterns
commonMisconceptions.misconception: string — the wrong belief, learner-voiced; quoted verbatim in UI

motivationHooks.hookId: string — NEW v6, optional. Lets sectionSummary.resolvesHookId and prediction-resolution hooks reference a specific opening hook.
motivationHooks.prompt: string — markdown permitted. NOTE: 'prediction-resolution' is NEW v6 — rendered at the END of the section (after the last experience, before the summary), explicitly resolving the opening prediction-gap. Pair via resolvesHookId.
motivationHooks.resolvesHookId: string — NEW v6, only on prediction-resolution hooks; the hookId of the prediction-gap being closed

sectionSummary.$comment: NEW v6, optional. Rendered as a recap card after the section's last experience. Closes the loop the section opened.
sectionSummary.content: string — markdown. 'What you should now believe' recap. Keep to 3–6 key claims.
sectionSummary.resolvesHookId: string — optional; the prediction-gap hookId this summary answers
keyTakeaways: string — optional; rendered as a checklist beneath the prose

learningExperiences.experienceId: string — unique; keys learner answers and review back-links
learningExperiences.title: string
learningExperiences.experienceType: guided-analysis | worked-example | case-study | simulation | discussion | self-guided-exercise
linkedCompetencies: competencyId — every scored event here records evidence against these
desiredCognitiveActions: prediction | analysis | reflection | retrieval | comparison | synthesis | elaboration

contentBlocks.$comment: V6 CONTENT MODEL. type gains: code, callout, table, key-terms. Text blocks gain format. Any block may set checkpointAfter. ALL author-supplied strings are sanitized by the player before injection (see doc §Security).
contentBlocks.type: text | code | callout | table | key-terms | image | video | audio | interactive
contentBlocks.checkpointAfter: boolean — NEW v6, optional, any block type. When any block in an experience sets this, the player renders the experience in progressive segments: content up to and including this block, then a 'Continue' checkpoint (with segment progress dots, e.g. '2 of 5'). Absent everywhere = single scroll, exactly as v5.
contentBlocks.format: markdown | plain — NEW v6, text blocks only. 'markdown' renders full CommonMark + GFM tables + fenced code. Default: 'markdown' when courseMetadata.schemaVersion >= 6.0, else 'plain'. Plain prose is valid markdown, so 'markdown' is always safe to set.
contentBlocks.content: string — for text: prose (markdown per format). For callout: body markdown. For interactive: label text. AUTHORING RULE (v6): never write pseudo-headings ('WHY THIS MATTERS: ...') inside prose; use markdown headings (##, ###), lists, and emphasis.
contentBlocks.language: string — code blocks only. Syntax-highlight language id (e.g. 'javascript', 'python', 'sql'). Optional; omit for plain code.
contentBlocks.caption: string — code and table blocks, optional
contentBlocks.highlightLines: integer[] — code blocks, optional; 1-based line numbers to emphasize
contentBlocks.variant: note | warning | insight | definition | example — callout blocks only. Controls the callout's color/icon treatment.
contentBlocks.title: string — callout blocks, optional heading
rows: string — table blocks: row cells; inline markdown permitted

terms.$comment: key-terms blocks only. Rendered as tappable definition chips.
terms.addToGlossary: boolean — optional, default true. When true the term is merged into the course glossary at load and auto-linked wherever it appears in rendered content.

interactionSequence.interactionType: prediction | compare-responses | reflection | elaboration | multiple-choice | drag-drop | scenario-analysis
interactionSequence.$elaborationNote: 'elaboration' is NEW v6: a free-text prompt placed AFTER an explanation or worked example, asking the learner to restate/apply in their own words ('In your own words, why does this matter for your current project?'). Scored as participation (0.15), same as reflection; distinct type exists so authors and analytics can distinguish generation-after-instruction from open reflection, and so the player can style it as a closing beat.
options: string — required for multiple-choice, drag-drop, likert
interactionSequence.correctIndex: integer — required for multiple-choice; drag-drop fallback key
correctOrder: integer — drag-drop: option indices best-to-worst; enables positional scoring

confidencePrompt.$comment: v6: EXECUTED (was dormant in v5 player). Rendered before submit when learningModel.confidenceCalibrationEnabled. The rating is stored in the answer record as {confidence: integer} and echoed in post-reveal calibration feedback ('You were 5/5 confident and incorrect'). Feeds scheduling when confidenceSchedulingEnabled.

steps: string — markdown permitted; fenced code allowed inside steps

fadedVariant.$comment: NEW v6, optional. Renders immediately after the worked example as a partially-completed version of the same problem class — the middle rung of worked → faded → independent. Learner fills the blanked steps as free text and self-checks against blankedStepAnswers on reveal. Scored as participation (0.15).
givenSteps: string — the steps provided to the learner
blankedStepPrompts: string — one prompt per step the learner must supply
blankedStepAnswers: string — model answers revealed after submission, index-aligned with blankedStepPrompts

verification.isGating: boolean — must be false; self-guided exercises are formative only

retrievalPractice.retrievalItemId: string — unique across the whole course; keys the review schedule

assessments.assessmentId: string — referenced by masteryVerification and desiredOutcomes.verifiedBy
assessments.title: string
linkedCompetencies: competencyId — all question scores land here
assessments.adaptiveDifficulty: boolean — v6: EXECUTED when true (was display-only). Questions are served one at a time, ordered by adaptiveRules; the badge now reflects real behavior.

adaptiveRules.$comment: NEW v6, optional; defaults apply when adaptiveDifficulty is true and this block is absent.
adaptiveRules.startDifficulty: integer 1–5 — default 2. First question is the first unanswered question at this difficulty.
adaptiveRules.promoteAfter: integer — default 2. Consecutive correct answers before stepping up one difficulty level.
adaptiveRules.demoteAfter: integer — default 1. Consecutive incorrect answers before stepping down one level.
adaptiveRules.servePerLevel: integer — default 2. Max questions served at a level before forced promotion (prevents stalling).
adaptiveRules.$note: Adaptive serving applies to auto-scorable questions (multiple-choice). Written questions are appended after the adaptive phase in authored order. When adaptiveDifficulty is false, all questions render at once in authored order, exactly as v5.

questions.questionId: string — unique within assessment. v6: also keys the missed-item review entry when reviewOnMiss is true.
questions.difficulty: integer 1–5 — v6: consumed by adaptive serving; still displayed
questions.prompt: string — markdown permitted; fenced code allowed
successCriteria: string — shown post-submit for written questions
questions.reviewPromptOverride: string — NEW v6, optional. When this question enters the review queue via reviewOnMiss, this text is used as the recall cue instead of the raw prompt. Use it to strip answer-revealing context (e.g. drop the options list).

retrievalSystem.algorithm: string — declarative; per-item schedule blocks drive the scheduler

masterySystem.decayModelEnabled: boolean — enforced; evidence weight halves per 30 days when true

analytics.calibrationTracking: boolean — NEW v6, optional. When true (and confidence is collected), the engine retains {confidence, score} pairs per event in the evidence ledger, enabling a calibration report (overconfidence/underconfidence by competency).

glossary.term: string — v6: terms are auto-linked on first occurrence in rendered text/callout content; tapping opens the glossary drawer filtered to the term. key-terms blocks with addToGlossary:true merge in at load (course-file glossary wins on term collisions).
